course_title: Identifying and Assisting Students at Risk

learning_outcomes.description: Increase your knowledge, understanding and skills in relation to the identification of children and adolescents at risk.
Increase your knowledge, understanding and skills in relation to the assessment of children and adolescents at risk, specifically your observation, interview, and screening skills.
Increase your knowledge, understanding and skills in relation to support for children and adolescents at risk.
Develop a working knowledge of major issues and approaches described in the literature, as it relates to the education of and support for students at risk.

assessments.due_date:
Powerpoint Presentation due by 14:00 24/08/2026 - 24/08/2026

Oral Simulation at scheduled time 24/08/2026 - 24/08/2026
assessments.task_description:
Work through the materials on the EDUC7001 BLACKBOARD site that refer to the case, ROBERT PAGE, a 14-year-old at-risk male high school student.

For the task, assume the role of your intended profession (e.g., teacher, guidance counsellor, social worker, educational psychologist, learning support teacher) where Robert Page attends school. As a stakeholder in the support team of Robert Page, you have been requested to present an overview of Robert’s case to the support team, discussing what has happened up to this point in his life to bring him to the attention of professionals.

This assessment aims to develop professionally relevant skills, to convey a deep understanding of the case of Robert Page. You will present a clear understanding of the risk and protective factors that may be influencing Robert’s behaviour and why these factors, according to literature, may be placing him at risk, or providing some protection for him.

Refer to the EDUC7001 Criteria Sheet Assignment 1 for the specific requirements for each section of the assignment and how the assignment will be assessed.

This live online oral simulation will be video recorded to ensure there is an accurate record of your performance. Recordings will be retained for 12 months on a secure UQ system in accordance with UQ assessment policy. For this online oral, you will require a working webcam, headphones, and you will be required to reveal the room during the oral.
assessments.submission_guidelines: A) The PowerPoint Presentation Slides: When you successfully submit your assessment to Turnitin you should receive a confirmation page as a digital receipt. You will receive this information via your student e-mail account. If you do not receive a submission ID you should regard your submission as unsuccessful.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Problem Based Learning: Identification, Assessment & Intervention

Task 1 will be released after class on Week 3. Due: 17/08/2026 2:00 pm

Task 2 will be released after class on Week 7. Due: 14/09/2026 2:00 pm

Task 3 will be released after class on Week 7. Due: 21/09/2026 2:00 pm

Task 4 will be released after class on Week 9. Due: 6/10/2026 2:00 pm
assessments.task_description:
You are to respond to a series of 4 mini tasks that demonstrates your understanding of core theoretical knowledge, key content, and concepts from course lectures and readings. These exercises are designed to help you apply core theoretical knowledge to a real-world student case and develop critical thinking skills and build your confidence in theory application.

Each task has a 500-word allowance and is worth 5% of the overall course grade. Tasks will be released progressively following the completion of relevant lecture content. You will have until 2pm the following Monday to post your response. All tasks will be assessed using a pass/fail rubric, with a focus on demonstrating understanding and application of key course concepts.

Each of the tasks is graded as a pass/fail. Students must successfully achieve a pass to receive the 5% allocated to each mini task in the series for a total of 20%.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission will be completed digitally, through Blackboard Ultra.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.weighting: <dd>60% 15 minute presentation with slides & 10 minute Q&A </dd>

assessments.category: Practical/ Demonstration, Presentation, Role play/ Simulation
assessments.task_description:
Work through the materials on the EDUC7001 BLACKBOARD site that refer to the case, ROBERT PAGE, a 14-year-old at-risk male high school student.

For the task, assume the role of your intended profession (e.g., teacher, guidance counsellor, social worker, educational psychologist, learning support teacher) where Robert Page attends school. As a stakeholder in the support team of Robert Page, you have been requested to provide and update of the case to your team members, as well as your proposed intervention plan to support Robert.

This assessment aims to develop professionally relevant skills, to convey a deep understanding of the case of Robert Page and produce a well thought out and detailed plan of action to support the implementation of a realistic intervention within the boundaries of your role.

Refer to the EDUC7001 Criteria Sheet Assignment 3 for the specific requirements for each section of the assignment and how the assignment will be assessed.

This live online oral simulation will be video recorded to ensure there is an accurate record of your performance. Recordings will be retained for 12 months on a secure UQ system in accordance with UQ assessment policy. For this online oral, you will require a working webcam, headphones, and you will be required to reveal the room during the oral.

This assessment task is to be completed live via video camera link. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools during the interview will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies in preparation, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.
assessments.submission_guidelines: A) The PowerPoint Presentation Slides: When you successfully submit your assessment to Turnitin you should receive a confirmation page as a digital receipt. You will receive this information via your student e-mail account. If you do not receive a submission ID you should regard your submission as unsuccessful.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
